title: Optimum estimator in simple random sampling using two auxiliary attributes with application in agriculture, fisheries and education sectors
author: Saini, Jitendrakumar, et al.

steps.title: Material and methods
steps.instruction: Consider ╬│=(╬│1,╬│2,╬│3,ÔÇĞ.,╬│N) be a finite population of size N. we draw a sample of size n (with n<N) units from ╬│ using simple random sample without replacement (SRSWOR). Let yi be the study variable and ß║şi be the characteristics of the auxiliary attributes i.e. ß║şi┬á=┬á1 if the ith unit possess attribute and ß║şi┬á=┬á0, otherwise. Let G=Ôêæi=1N╬┤ibe the total number of units in the population possessing attributes ß║şi and g=Ôêæi=1n╬┤ibe the total number of units in the sample possessing attributes ß║şi. Let D=(G/N) be the proportion of units in the population and d=(g/n) be the population of units in the sample. Let Y┬»=Ôêæi=1NyiNand y┬»=Ôêæi=1nyinbe the population and sample mean of the study variable. Let D1 and D2 be the population proportion of the auxiliary attributes and sample proportion of auxiliary attributes is denoted by d1 and d2. Let Sy2=Ôêæi=1N(yiÔêÆY┬»)2NÔêÆ1be the population variance of the study variable y. Let Sd12=Ôêæi=1N(d1ÔêÆD1)2NÔêÆ1and Sd22=Ôêæi=1N(d2ÔêÆD2)2NÔêÆ1respectively be the population variance of the auxiliary attributes d1 and d2. Let Cy=SyY┬» be the coefficient of variation of the study variable y. Let Cd1=Sd1D1 and Cd2=Sd2D2 be the coefficient of variation of the auxiliary d1 and d2. Let Sydj=Ôêæi=1N(yiÔêÆY┬»)(djÔêÆDj)NÔêÆ1be the population covariance between the study variable y and the auxiliary attributes dj(j=1,2). Let Sd1d2=Ôêæi=1N(d1ÔêÆD1)(d2ÔêÆD2)NÔêÆ1be the population covariance between the auxiliary attributes d1 and d2. Let ¤üydj=SydjSySdj be the population point bi-serial correlation coefficient between the study variable y and the auxiliary attribute dj(j=1,2).Let ¤Ç0=y┬»ÔêÆY┬»Y┬»; ¤Ç1=d1ÔêÆD1D1 and ¤Ç2=d2ÔêÆD2D2 be the error terms such that E[ei]=0(i=0,1,2), E[¤Ç02]=╬¥Cy2,E[¤Ç12]=╬¥Cd12,E[¤Ç22]=╬¥Cd22,E[¤Ç0¤Ç1]=╬¥¤üyd1CyCd1,E[¤Ç0¤Ç2]=╬¥¤üyd2CyCd2andE[¤Ç1¤Ç2]=╬¥¤üd1d2Cd1Cd2where ╬¥=(1nÔêÆ1N) and f=nN .

steps.title: Material and methods
steps.instruction: The stepwise framework of proposed method is described as follows:Step 1: Consider a finite population of size N.Step 2: Select a random sample of size n from the population using simple random sampling without replacement.Step 3: Observe yi and ß║şi from sampling units.Step 4: Define the expressions for population and sample characteristics.Step 5: Propose the estimator for estimating population mean using two auxiliary attributes and derive its properties.Step 6: Compare the proposed estimator with the existing estimators theoretically and numerically.
